name: 物种形成
subject: biology
domain_name: Unit 7-8 进化与生态 Evolution & Ecology
curriculum_points:
BIG IDEA 1: EVOLUTION
The process of evolution drives the diversity and unity of life. Evolution is a change in the genetic makeup of a population over time, with natural selection as its major driving mechanism. Darwin’s theory, which is supported by evidence from many scientific disciplines, states that inheritable variations occur in individuals in a population. Due to competition for limited resources, individuals with more favorable genetic variations are more likely to survive and produce more offspring, thus passing traits to future generations.

7.2 Natural Selection
Explain how natural selection affects populations.
7.2.A.1
Natural selection is a major mechanism of evolution. Natural selection acts on phenotypic variations in populations.
7.2.A.2
Evolution is also driven by random occurrences. Mutations are random and are not directed by environmental or organismal needs. Some mutations are beneficial, increasing the fitness of the organism, while others are harmful, decreasing the fitness of the organism. Mutations are the primary source of new genetic variation in a population.

7.2.A.3
Environments change and apply selective pressures to populations. Evolution occurs when the frequency of alleles in a population changes over time due to natural selection. The unequal survival and reproduction of organisms with different phenotypes, caused by natural selection, can lead to the evolution of populations.

7.2.B
Explain how natural selection affects individuals and populations.
7.2.B.1
Organisms with heritable traits that are better suited to their environment tend to survive and produce more offspring than organisms with less suitable traits. This differential survival and reproduction is the process of natural selection.
